priorities.label: B1 Host-migration D2 (instrument-only): execute the parked, 3-way-deliberated PDR 2026-07-04_PDR_S116_HostMig_D2.md verbatim. Epoch envelope field + snapshot-starvation detector + pure computeSuccessorSeat + live client-pubkey/HELLO wiring + PoP + warrant@Begin. 8 files + new src/net/succession.ts + ~35 tests. All additive-optional -> PROTOCOL_VERSION held 14. Gemini fix: forward-looking worker postMessage seam comment in succession.ts.
priorities.check_method: B1 host-migration D2 shipped (637ed84), instrument-only detection layer, executed the parked 3-way-deliberated PDR verbatim + A.0-re-verified @ bfcadda. 8 files + new succession.ts + 4 new test files (32 tests). PROTOCOL_VERSION held 14 (all fields additive-optional). succession.ts: pure computeSuccessorSeat (lowest warranted+alive; MIXED-BUILD tolerant) + isSnapshotStarved + HOST_STARVATION_MS 6000 + worker-boundary forward-comment (Gemini Q1). PoP (Grok W1): buildPubkeyPopPayload + verifyPubkeyPop bind (room,peer,spki) -> closes claim-any-pubkey + anti-replay; host stores peerPubkeys only after PoP passes. protocol.ts: HELLO.clientPubkeyPopB64? + NETSNAPSHOT.epoch? + START_GAME_SIGNAL.warrant? all fail-closed + isValidWarrant. sync.ts: HostSync stamps epoch (OMITTED at 0 -> wire byte-identical; provably inert ClientSync gate 0<0 false) + lastAcceptedAt + setEpoch (D3-ready). hostHandlers: warrant SIGNED BEFORE Begin broadcast (Grok ordering-by-construction) wrapped fail-open (RALPH:PATROL added try/catch so a sign failure can't block Begin). main.ts: boot client identity + edge-triggered starvation detector w/ visibilityState forensics (Gemini FIX 1). GATE: tsc 0; vitest 1814/1814 (+32); save.replay 24/24 byte-identical; build clean bundle 622.6/750 KiB. RALPH:PATROL PASS. Instrument-only: crypto is host-side, forensics client-side, NOTHING enters sim/save. CARRY-FORWARD (D3): MIGRATION_CLAIM takeover + transport-grounded alive set + epoch-advance/reset rules (D4).

priorities.label: B3 Keystone Anchor symbiotic combo: an Anchor bond confers its territorial-rigidity floor to magic bonds bonded to its endpoint prims. New src/state/keystoneAnchor.ts + test; wire into stepPhysics AFTER applyAnchorStabilize; new KEYSTONE_STIFFNESS_FLOOR const. Replay-safe by construction (ephemeral stiffnessMultiplier, idempotent constant-floor max, host-only). PROTOCOL_VERSION held 14, save.replay byte-identical.
priorities.check_method: B3 Keystone Anchor shipped (5df5d4b) — first symbiotic-chaining combo (audit F8 proof-of-vision, Council S118 Q2 ADOPT). New state/keystoneAnchor.ts applyKeystoneAnchor(world): un-fouled Anchor (Dot<->Square) confers KEYSTONE_STIFFNESS_FLOOR (0.5, below anchor's own 0.7) to MAGIC bonds bonded to its endpoint prims. Wired in physicsLoop AFTER applyAnchorStabilize, BEFORE substeps. REPLAY-SAFE BY CONSTRUCTION: per-bond idempotent constant-floor max() -> iteration-order-irrelevant (PRIME-AUDIT of Grok's sort-IDs fix confirmed unnecessary); stiffnessMultiplier ephemeral (save.ts skips) -> ZERO wire/save bytes, PROTOCOL_VERSION untouched. Only lifts SAGGED magic neighbors; skips fouled source/target; never lowers (an anchor-neighbor keeps its higher 0.7). 11-test keystoneAnchor.test.ts: both-endpoint conferral (Capsule off Square + Filament off Dot), functional/no-anchor non-conferral, never-lower, fouled source/target skip, anchor-untouched, idempotent, order-independent. GATE: tsc 0; vitest 1825/1825 (+11); save.replay 24/24 byte-identical; build clean bundle 623.4/750 KiB. RALPH:PATROL PASS. CARRY-FORWARD (Gemini Q2 salience): VFX telegraph + income-based 2nd symbiotic combo.

priorities.check_method: F1b/F2 territory perf shipped (06de193) — closes the deferred half of audit F1. Replaced per-player componentOf BFS (rebuilt per unvisited prim, per-player per-tick) with ONE global union-find labeling pass (computeComponentRoots, union-by-MIN-id -> canonical partition) reused across all players; computeAllPlayerComplexities (one prim pass + one bond pass bucketed by color, final expression VERBATIM) + computeAllPlayerRadii; computePlayerComplexity/computeTerritorialRadius are thin wrappers (single source of truth); computeTerritorialInfluence + isInsideEnemyTerritory compute the radius map ONCE (F2). BYTE-IDENTICAL BY CONSTRUCTION (Sym-D -> single-color components -> distinct global roots among a color == old per-player count; identical integer counts -> identical float expr). COUNCIL S118 Q3 GATE-STRENGTHENED: (a) audited all ~15 componentOf callers FIRST -> only territory counts, no downstream label-identity dependence, componentOf itself UNCHANGED -> Gemini's relabeling hazard cannot occur; (b) territory.differential.test.ts (400 random worlds) asserts canonical partition + complexity + radius + FULL post-influence stiffness map all bit-exact (Object.is) vs a componentOf-derived reference. GATE: tsc 0; vitest 1826/1826; differential 400/400 bit-exact; territory.test.ts 21/21 regression; save.replay byte-identical; build clean bundle 624.0/750 KiB; PROTOCOL_VERSION untouched. RALPH:PATROL PASS.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: S118-P1 #execute-a-deliberated-parked-pdr-verbatim-but-re-run-A.0: the hostmig-D2 PDR was fully 3-way-deliberated in S116 then PARKED; S117 landed in between. Rather than re-deliberate, re-ran ONLY the A.0 state-discovery gate (net/ untouched by S117: D1 primitives present, succession.ts absent, no epoch, v14) -> the PDR's CURRENT-STATE block still held, so it executed verbatim. LESSON: a parked deliberated PDR carries its deliberation; the cheap, mandatory re-check is A.0 (has external state drifted?), not the whole Council.

S118-P1 #ralph-patrol-caught-async-refactor-blocking-the-happy-path: signing the warrant BEFORE Begin (Grok ordering-by-construction) made beginMatch async with an await before the START_GAME dispatch -> a signWarrant throw would have silently prevented the match from starting. RALPH:PATROL flagged it; wrapped fail-open (log + proceed sans warrant). LESSON: when a correctness-ordering fix turns a sync happy-path async, audit what a mid-path reject/throw now blocks.

S118-P2 #reuse-the-proven-replay-safe-template-for-the-cheap-proof: B3 (first symbiotic combo) deliberately extended the EXISTING applyAnchorStabilize pattern (ephemeral per-tick stiffnessMultiplier, idempotent constant-floor max) rather than inventing an income mechanic that would touch the byte-identity-sensitive scoring path. Result: replay-safe BY CONSTRUCTION, zero wire/save bytes, PROTOCOL_VERSION untouched, save.replay 24/24 green on the first run. LESSON: for a cheap proof-of-vision, pick the insertion seam whose determinism/replay-safety is already proven by a shipped sibling — novelty in the DESIGN (symbiotic conferral), not in the RISK surface.

S118-P3 #the-caller-audit-was-the-real-gate-not-the-differential: the Council's convergent REJECT of F1b's naive gate resolved not by a fancier test but by a 2-minute componentOf-caller audit that found F1b does NOT modify componentOf (only adds a labeling internal to territory's count) + no caller consumes territory's labels -> Gemini's downstream-ID-key hazard was structurally impossible. The 400-world differential (canonical partition + complexity + radius + full stiffness map) then CONFIRMED byte-identity empirically. LESSON: when a reviewer flags a relabeling hazard, first check whether the shared primitive is even touched + who consumes its identity; a scoping audit can dissolve a hazard that a test would only sample.

S118-P4 #deploy-last-makes-the-deploy-meaningful: sequenced deploy as P4 (after B1+B3+F1b/F2) rather than deploying the S117 build first, so ONE deploy carries real player-facing change (reliability forensics + a new combo) instead of a no-op S117 push. The deploy script's own live-hash verify + a post-deploy HTTP smoke (index 200 + bundle 200 exact-byte-match + apex 200) confirmed it without needing a browser. LESSON: when several priorities will ship together, defer the outward-facing deploy to last so it is a single meaningful release, and lean on the deploy script's built-in hash-verify + a cheap HTTP smoke for confirmation.

analyze_retrospective: S118 ANALYZE — Tier-B batch 4/4 shipped + deployed, all gates green (tsc 0, vitest 1826/1826, F1b differential 400/400 bit-exact, save.replay 24/24 byte-identical, bundle 624.0/750, PROTOCOL_VERSION 14 held). CALIBRATION: deliberation was high-value on the NEW surface, low-cost elsewhere — B1 carried its parked 3-way (only A.0 re-run needed); the focused 3-way earned its keep twice: (1) F1b convergent REJECT forced the componentOf-caller audit that dissolved the relabeling hazard + the 400-world differential that empirically confirmed byte-identity; (2) RALPH:PATROL caught the async-Begin refactor blocking the happy-path (fail-open fix). PRIME-AUDIT REFUTED two plausible-but-wrong Council criticals (Grok sort-IDs on B3, Grok root-selection on F1b) — the #empirical-refutes-plausible-criticals pattern (now 6x, a promotion candidate) held again. NO REGRESSIONS. Deferred B2 worker-sim deliberately (large/undeliberated) — the right best-interest trade: shipped 3 complete, verified, deployed wins rather than one rushed worker migration. Model: ran on Opus 4.8 (owner override of Fable-5 pin) — drift warning persists, non-blocking. Next session: B2 worker-sim (its own PDR), then host-mig D3 (MIGRATION_CLAIM takeover, building on this D2 detection layer).
